title_en: AI Isn’t Coming for Hollywood. It's Already Arrived
title_zh: AI并未“来袭”好莱坞，它早已到来

paragraphs.en: Lady Gaga probably wasn’t thinking that a coup would unfold in her greenhouse. Then again, she was cohosting a party there with Sean Parker, the billionaire founder of Napster and first president of Facebook.
paragraphs.zh: Lady Gaga 大概没想到，一场“政变”会在她的温室里上演。更何况，当时她正和 Sean Parker（Napster 的亿万富翁创始人、Facebook 的首任董事长）一起在那里共办派对。

paragraphs.en: It was February 2024, and the singer had invited guests to her $22.5 million oceanside estate in Malibu to mark the launch of a skin-care nonprofit. One of the organization’s trustees was her boyfriend, whose day job was running the Parker Foundation. In the candlelit space, beside floor-to-ceiling windows that looked out over the Pacific, Parker’s people mingled with Gaga’s, nibbling focaccia and branzino alla brace to music from a string quartet (Grammy-winning, of course).
paragraphs.zh: 那是 2024 年 2 月，歌手邀请宾客来到她位于马里布、价值 2250 万美元的海边庄园，庆祝一家护肤公益组织的启动活动。该机构的一位理事是她的男友，他平时的工作是在运营 Parker Foundation。烛光映照的场地里，在一面面面向太平洋的落地窗旁，Parker 一方和 Gaga 一方的人士边吃薄脆面包（focaccia）和炭烤鳕鱼（branzino alla brace），边听一支弦乐四重奏（当然是格莱美获奖团队）演奏。

paragraphs.en: Prem Akkaraju, one of Parker’s close friends and business partners, arrived in a tailored suit, his thick hair coifed to perfection. The two men had known each other since Parker was at Facebook and Akkaraju was in the music industry. Over the years, they’d tried unsuccessfully to launch a movie streaming platform together and—much more successfully—had taken over a renowned visual effects company. Lately they had been talking about starting an AI venture.
paragraphs.zh: Parker 的亲密朋友之一、也是商业伙伴的 Prem Akkaraju 穿着合身的定制西装出现了，浓密的头发打理得一丝不苟。这两位男士早在 Parker 在 Facebook 任职、Akkaraju 从事音乐产业时期就彼此认识。多年来，他们曾一起尝试创建影像流媒体平台，未能成功；但却在一件事情上成功得多得多——接管了一家著名的视觉效果公司。最近，他们在讨论启动一个 AI 项目。

paragraphs.en: That evening at Gaga’s, Akkaraju found himself sitting next to an investor in Stability AI, the company that launched the wildly popular text-to-image generator Stable Diffusion in 2022. Despite its early success, Stability was “circling the drain,” the investor recalls. It was “within days of not having options.” He told Akkaraju: “You should take Stability and make it into the Hollywood-friendly AI model.”
paragraphs.zh: 那晚在 Gaga 的派对上，Akkaraju 坐在了 Stability AI 的一位投资人旁边。该公司在 2022 年推出了广受欢迎的文本生成图像模型 Stable Diffusion。尽管早期成绩斐然，这位投资人回忆起当时，Stability 几近“走到尽头”。它“几乎已经到了没有退路、寸步难行”的阶段。于是他对 Akkaraju 说："你应该把 Stability 接过来，做成 Hollywood 友好的 AI 模型。"

paragraphs.en: Hollywood did seem to be in need of a friend. Since 2022, the number of films and TV shows made in the United States had dropped by about 40 percent, thanks to ballooning production costs at home, competition from overseas, and long-running labor disputes everywhere. AI promised to bring the numbers back up by speeding production and slashing costs: Let computers automate the grunt work of translating dialog, adding visual effects frame by painstaking frame, and editing boom microphones out of a zillion shots. Maybe one day they could even write scripts and act! Two of the industry’s biggest unions had gone on strike in part to obtain assurances that generative AI wouldn’t replace union jobs in the near term. But every major studio and streaming service was racing to figure out its AI strategy, and a host of startups—Luma, Runway, Asteria—was working on tools to pitch them.
paragraphs.zh: 好莱坞似乎确实需要一个“朋友”。自2022年以来，美国制作的电影和电视剧数量下降了约40%，原因是国内制作成本飙升、海外竞争加剧，以及长期不断的劳资纠纷。人工智能曾被寄予厚望，认为它能加快制作流程、削减成本，从而把数字拉回来：让计算机自动化处理枯燥的对白翻译工作、将视觉特效一帧一帧地逐步添加、把成千上万个镜头里夸张大幅增益麦克风轨道删去。也许总有一天，它们甚至能写剧本和表演！该行业两大最大的工会部分发起罢工，也是为了争取保障，确保生成式人工智能不会在短期内取代工会岗位。与此同时，每一家大型电影公司和流媒体平台都在竞相制定自己的AI策略，而一批初创公司——如Luma、Runway、Asteria——也在开发用于推销给它们的相关工具。

paragraphs.en: Akkaraju saw the opportunity in front of him. Stability AI had the technology. It just needed that Hollywood finish. As far as he could tell, there was only one problem. Didn’t the company already have a CEO?
paragraphs.zh: Akkaraju看到了摆在面前的机会。Stability AI手里已经有了技术。它只需要把事情推进到好莱坞这边。依他观察，几乎只有一个问题。那公司不是已经有一位CEO了吗？

paragraphs.en: When Emad Mostaque, a former hedge fund manager, founded Stability in 2020, the company’s mission was to “build systems that make a real difference” in solving society's toughest problems. By 2022, the system Mostaque felt he needed to build was a cloud supercomputer powerful enough to run a generative AI model. OpenAI was gaining traction with its closed-source models, and Mostaque wanted to make an open source alternative—“like Linux to Windows,” he says. He offered up the supercomputer to a group of academic researchers working on an open source system where you could type words to generate an image. The researchers weren’t going to say no. In August of that year, they launched Stable Diffusion in partnership with Mostaque’s company.
paragraphs.zh: 2020年，前对冲基金经理Emad Mostaque创立了Stability时，公司的使命是“构建能够在解决社会最棘手问题上产生真正影响的系统”。到2022年，Mostaque觉得自己需要构建的是一台足够强大的云端超级计算机，以运行生成式人工智能模型。OpenAI的封闭模型正在走强，而Mostaque希望做一个开源替代品——他称之为“像Linux对Windows那样”。他将这台超级计算机提供给一群在开发开源系统的学术研究者使用，这个系统允许人们输入文字就能生成图像。研究者们没有拒绝。那年8月，在Mostaque公司与他们的合作下，Stable Diffusion正式上线。

paragraphs.en: The text-to-image generator was a breakout hit, garnering 10 million users in two months. “It was fairly close to state-of-the-art,” says Maneesh Agrawala, a computer science professor at Stanford University. Openness was core to the model’s success. “It allowed researchers to essentially extend the model, fine-tune it, and it spurred a whole community into action in terms of creating enhancements and add-ons,” Agrawala says. By October 2022, Stability AI had only 77 employees, but with thousands of times that many people in the wider Stable Diffusion community, it could compete with its bigger rivals. Mostaque raised $101 million in a seed round from venture capital firms and hedge funds including Coatue and Lightspeed (the final million, he tells me, was for good luck). The company was a unicorn.
paragraphs.zh: 这个文字生成图像的工具一炮而红，两个月内就吸引了1000万用户。“它当时已经相当接近当下的最先进水平，”斯坦福大学计算机科学教授Maneesh Agrawala说。开放性是该模型成功的核心。“它让研究者能够实质性地扩展模型、进行微调，并且激发了整个社区在创建改进和插件方面积极行动起来。”Agrawala说。到2022年10月，Stability AI只有77名员工，但在更广泛的Stable Diffusion社区中有成千上万的参与者，因此它能够与更大的竞争对手抗衡。Mostaque从包括Coatue和Lightspeed在内的风险投资机构与对冲基金融资中，完成了1.01亿美元的种子轮融资（他告诉我，最后一百万是“给好运气”的）。这家公司已经成为了一家独角兽企业。

paragraphs.en: Employees from this period describe Mostaque as a visionary. He spoke eloquently about the need to democratize access to artificial intelligence. In the not-too-distant future, Mostaque told employees, the company would solve complex biomedical problems and generate season eight of Game of Thrones. “It was an incredibly fun and chaotic startup that was throwing a lot of spaghetti at the wall, and some of it stuck really hard,” a former high-ranking employee tells me. (Like others I spoke with, the employee requested anonymity to speak freely about Mostaque and the company.)
paragraphs.zh: 这段时期的员工把Mostaque形容为一位有远见的人。他在谈论普及人工智能的重要性时，能说得很有感染力。在不久的将来，Mostaque告诉员工，公司将会解决复杂的生物医学问题，并生成《权力的游戏》第八季的内容。“那是一家非常有趣、非常混乱的初创公司，像是往墙上砸了一大堆意大利面，结果其中一些竟然黏得非常牢固，”一名前高层员工告诉我。（像我采访的其他人一样，这位员工要求匿名，以便可以更自由地谈论Mostaque和公司。）

paragraphs.en: Mostaque was thrilled by the success. But he was also in over his head. “I was brand-new to this,” he says. “With my Aspergers and ADHD, I was like, ‘What's going on?’” Mostaque talks fast, his tone matter-of-fact: “On the research side, we did really good things. The other side I was not so good at, which was the management side.” Two former employees told me that they felt Mostaque didn’t think deeply about building a marketable product. “He just wanted to build models,” one said.
paragraphs.zh: Mostaque对成功感到欣喜，但他也有些力不从心。“我对此几乎是个门外汉，”他说。“由于我有阿斯伯格综合征和注意力缺陷多动障碍，我只想：‘这到底是怎么回事？’”Mostaque说话很快，语气冷静而平实：“在研发方面，我们确实做了很好的事。另一边我不擅长，也就是管理方面。”两名前员工告诉我，他们觉得Mostaque没有深入思考如何打造一款可销售的产品。“他只想要打造模型，”其中一人说。

paragraphs.en: The company’s success brought heightened scrutiny. In January 2023, Getty Images sued Stability AI in London’s High Court for allegedly training its models on 12 million proprietary photographs. The company filed a similar suit in the US weeks later. In the stateside complaint, Getty accused the AI firm of “brazen theft and freeriding.”
paragraphs.zh: 公司的成功也带来了更严格的审视。2023年1月，Getty Images在伦敦高等法院起诉Stability AI，指控其在12百万张专有照片上训练模型。该公司几周后在美国也提起了类似诉讼。在这份美国起诉书中，Getty指责该AI公司“肆意窃取且搭便车”。

paragraphs.en: Then, in June 2023, Forbes published a blockbuster story alleging that Mostaque had inflated his credentials and misrepresented the business in pitch decks to his investors. The article also claimed that Mostaque had received only a bachelor’s degree from Oxford, not a master’s. (Mostaque says that he earned both, but a clerical error on his part was responsible for the mix-up.) What’s more, Stability reportedly owed millions of dollars to Amazon Web Services, which provided the computing power for its model. Though Mostaque had spoken of a partnership, Stability’s spokesperson acknowledged to Forbes that it was in fact a run-of-the-mill cloud services agreement with a standard discount.
paragraphs.zh: 随后在2023年6月，Forbes刊登了一篇爆款报道，称Mostaque夸大了个人资历，并在路演材料中向投资人误导性地夸大业务。报道还称Mostaque只获得了牛津大学的学士学位，而不是硕士学位。（Mostaque表示他确实拿到了两者，但他承认这是他那边的文书错误导致了混淆。）此外，报道称Stability拖欠Amazon Web Services数百万美元，而后者为其模型提供了计算力。尽管Mostaque曾谈及合作伙伴关系，Stability的发言人向Forbes承认，实际只是与其签订了带有标准折扣的普通云服务协议。

paragraphs.en: Mostaque had answers for all of this, but investors lost confidence anyway. Four months after the article came out, VCs from both Coatue and Lightspeed left the board of directors, signaling they no longer had faith in the business. By the end of the year, the company’s head of research, chief operating officer, general counsel, and head of human resources had left as well. Many of Stability’s prominent researchers would follow. Under pressure from investors, Mostaque finally left the company on March 22, 2024—just a few weeks after Lady Gaga’s greenhouse soiree.
paragraphs.zh: Mostaque 对此都有了应对办法，但投资者依旧失去了信心。文章发布四个月后，Coatue 和 Lightspeed 的风险投资机构相继退出了董事会，这表明他们已不再信任这家公司的业务。到年底，公司研究负责人、首席运营官、总法律顾问和人力资源负责人也都离职。Stability 的许多知名研究人员随后也纷纷离开。在投资者压力下，Mostaque 最终于 2024 年 3 月 22 日离开公司——就在 Lady Gaga 温室派对之后仅几周的时间。

paragraphs.en: Akkaraju and Parker wasted no time in taking over Stability, installing Akkaraju as CEO and Parker as chairman of the board. They never spoke to Mostaque, although the former CEO says he reached out to offer his support.
paragraphs.zh: Akkaraju 与 Parker 立即接管了 Stability，Akkaraju 出任首席执行官，Parker 成为董事会主席。据了解，他们从未与 Mostaque 交谈，尽管前任 CEO 表示自己曾主动联系过并表示愿意提供支持。

paragraphs.en: The pair set about trying to remake Stability AI for the moment. Not long after they took over, the competition got fiercer. That September, another startup, Runway, signed the AI industry’s first big deal with a movie studio. Runway would get access to Lionsgate’s proprietary catalog of movies as training data and develop tools for the studio. “The time it takes to go from idea to execution is just shrinking—like a lot,” says Cristóbal Valenzuela, CEO of Runway. “You can do things in just a couple of minutes that used to take two weeks.” In the coming years, he predicts, “you will have teams of two, three, four people making the work that used to require armies and hundreds of millions of dollars.”
paragraphs.zh: 这对搭档随即着手将 Stability 进行“按当下形势”改造。接管后不久，竞争变得更为激烈。9 月，另一家初创公司 Runway 与一家电影公司签下了人工智能行业的首个大型合作协议。Runway 将获得 Lionsgate 的专有电影目录作为训练数据，并为该电影公司开发工具。Runway 首席执行官 Cristóbal Valenzuela 表示：“从想法到落地所需的时间正在不断缩短——缩得很多。你现在能在几分钟内完成的事，过去可能要花两周。”他预测，未来几年，“过去需要一支队伍、几百万人力和上亿美元投入的工作，将由两到四个人的团队完成。”

paragraphs.en: The deal with Lionsgate pushed the AI-fication of Hollywood into overdrive. “I can tell you, last year when I came to Los Angeles versus today, it’s night and day,” says Amit Jain, CEO of Luma, another Stability competitor. “Last year it was ‘Let’s prototype, let’s proof-of-concept’—they were deferring the inevitable. This year it’s a whole different tone.”
paragraphs.zh: 与 Lionsgate 的交易进一步把好莱坞的“AI 化”推向了快车道。Luma 的首席执行官 Amit Jain——另一家 Stability 的竞争对手——说：“我可以告诉你，去年我来洛杉矶和今天相比，完全是天壤之别。”他补充道：“去年大家还在说‘先做原型，先做概念验证’——他们只是把不可避免的事情暂时搁置。到了今年，语气就完全不一样了。”

paragraphs.en: Moonvalley, an AI company founded by former Google DeepMind researchers (and the parent company of Asteria, an AI film studio cofounded by the actor Natasha Lyonne), recently told Time magazine that more than a dozen major Hollywood studios are testing its latest model—signaling openness to the technology, if not yet a full embrace.
paragraphs.zh: 由前 Google DeepMind 研究人员创立的 AI 公司 Moonvalley（以及由演员 Natasha Lyonne 共同创立的 AI 电影工作室 Asteria 的母公司）最近告诉《时代》杂志称，已有十几家主要好莱坞制片厂正在测试其最新模型，这表明行业对这项技术持开放态度，即使还没有完全全面采用。

paragraphs.en: “It was really about me and Sean coming in and providing that direction, that leadership, and really taking advantage of what we call the three T’s: timing, team, and technology,” Akkaraju says.
paragraphs.zh: Akkaraju 说：“这主要是我和 Sean 一起上场，提供方向、提供领导，并真正利用我们称之为三大 T 的东西：时机（timing）、团队（team）和技术（technology）。”

paragraphs.en: I’m sitting not at his TED Talk but in his $20 million mansion near Beverly Hills, on an immaculate overstuffed white couch overlooking a manicured garden. Akkaraju is fit, with a gleaming white smile and a button-up that shows off his biceps. His eye contact and handshake are equally strong.
paragraphs.zh: 我不是在听他做 TED 演讲，而是在他位于比弗利山附近的价值两千万美元的豪宅里，坐在一张洁白、看起来舒适度极高的沙发上，透过沙发可以俯瞰一座修剪得很整齐的花园。Akkaraju 看起来很健壮，笑容明亮，穿着一件衬衫把他的二头肌展示得很明显。他的目光接触和握手都同样有力。

paragraphs.en: Early on in his tenure, Akkaraju says, he decided that Stability would no longer compete with OpenAI and Google on building frontier models. Instead, it would create apps that sat on top of those models, freeing the company from enormous computing costs. Akkaraju negotiated a new deal with Stability AI’s cloud computing vendors, wiping away the company’s massive debt. Asked for specifics on how this came about, Akkaraju, through a spokesperson, demurred. Investors, like Coatue, came flocking back.
paragraphs.zh: 在他任职初期，Akkaraju 说，他决定让 Stability 不再与 OpenAI 和 Google 在前沿模型上正面竞争。相反，公司将着重开发运行在这些模型之上的应用，从而摆脱巨额算力成本。Akkaraju 与 Stability AI 的云计算供应商重新谈判，清除了公司的庞大债务。记者向他追问具体如何做到这一点时，Akkaraju 通过发言人含糊其辞。像 Coatue 这样的投资者纷纷回流。

paragraphs.en: Where Mostaque painted a picture of AI solving the world’s most difficult problems, what Akkaraju is building, in brutally unsexy terms, is a software-as-a-service company for Hollywood. The goal is not to generate films, he says, but to use AI to augment the tools that filmmakers already use. “I really do think that our differentiation is having the creator in the center,” Akkaraju says. “I don't see any other AI company that has James Cameron on its board.”
paragraphs.zh: 当Mostaque描绘出一幅AI解决世界上最难问题的宏大蓝图时，Akkaraju正在做的事情——用赤裸直白的方式说——其实是在为好莱坞打造一家软件即服务公司。Akkaraju 说，目标不是“生成电影”，而是用人工智能增强电影人已经在使用的工具。“我真的认为我们的差异化就在于把创作者放在中心位置，”Akkaraju说，“我没见过其他哪家AI公司有James Cameron坐进董事会。”

paragraphs.en: Yes, the irony writes itself: The guy who once had a fever dream about murderous machines while “sick and broke” in Rome and proceeded to turn it into The Terminator—the creator of Skynet!—is on the board of an AI company. What’s doubly surprising, though, is that Cameron is on the board of an AI company run by Parker and Akkaraju. A decade ago, Cameron was helping lead Hollywood’s charge against them. He didn’t appreciate the premise of their streaming platform, the Screening Room, which let people watch new releases at home for $50 on the same day they came out in theaters. Cameron reportedly told a crowd at CinemaCon that he was “committed to the theater experience.” In the years that followed, none of the major studios publicly announced deals with the Screening Room, and in 2020 the company rebranded as SR Labs.
paragraphs.zh: 是的，讽刺得不言自明：这个人曾经在罗马“病入膏肓、身无分文”时，做过一个关于“杀人机器”的狂想梦，后来又把它变成了《终结者》——天网（Skynet）的创造者——如今却成为一家AI公司的董事。而更令人惊讶的是，Cameron还是由Parker和Akkaraju经营的AI公司的董事会成员。十年前，Cameron还在主导好莱坞对他们的反对。当时他不看好他们的流媒体平台Screening Room的前提：让人们在电影上映当日以50美元在家观看新片。Cameron据报道在CinemaCon上对观众说过自己“致力于影院体验”。在接下来的几年里，没有哪一家主流电影公司公开宣布与Screening Room达成合作，而在2020年该公司更名为SR Labs。

paragraphs.en: That same year, Akkaraju and Parker took over Weta Digital, the visual effects studio behind blockbusters such as Lord of the Rings, Game of Thrones, and Cameron’s Avatar movies. Weta developed virtual cameras that let Cameron see a real-time rendering of the artificial environment through a viewfinder, as if he were filming on location in the fictional world of Pandora.
paragraphs.zh: 就在同一年，Akkaraju和Parker接管了Weta Digital，这家视觉特效公司参与制作过《指环王》《权力的游戏》以及Cameron的《阿凡达》系列等大片。Weta开发出了虚拟摄影机，让Cameron可以通过取景器实时看到人工构建环境的画面，就像他在潘多拉世界的虚拟拍摄现场拍摄一样。

paragraphs.en: One night, Cameron, Akkaraju, and Parker met for dinner to discuss how technology was changing the film industry. “The tequila was flowing,” Cameron recalls. “A friendship formed.” Any tension that had existed over the Screening Room melted away. (“I never really talked with him about it,” Akkaraju says. “He knew, and I knew. It was very funny.”)
paragraphs.zh: 有一个晚上，Cameron、Akkaraju和Parker围坐共进晚餐，讨论科技如何改变电影行业。“龙舌兰酒不断上桌，”Cameron回忆道，“于是形成了一段友谊。”此前围绕Screening Room存在的任何紧张气氛都消散了。“我从未真正和他谈过这件事，”Akkaraju说，“他也明白，我也明白。那真是很有趣。”

paragraphs.en: So Cameron is on the board, but is the “creator in the center,” as Akkaraju said? When I spoke with Parker, he emphasized the importance of using open source models and spoke of “respect for creators and respect for IP.” He added: “That sounds potentially kind of rich, coming from me, given my past association with Napster and early social media. But it is a lesson learned.”
paragraphs.zh: 所以卡梅隆在董事会里有席位，但他真的是阿卡拉朱所说的“以创作者为中心”了吗？当我与帕克交谈时，他强调了使用开源模型的重要性，并提到“对创作者要有尊重，对知识产权要有尊重”。他补充说：“考虑到我过去与 Napster 和早期社交媒体的关联，这听起来可能有点讽刺，但这是一堂刻骨铭心的教训。”

paragraphs.en: In June, the company scored a major win when Getty dropped its copyright infringement claims from a broader lawsuit as the trial neared a close in the UK. The US trial is ongoing. Akkaraju said the company “sources data from publicly available and licensed datasets for training and fine-tuning,” and that when “creating solutions for a client” it “fine-tunes using the dataset provided by the client.” When I asked Akkaraju if the company trained exclusively on free or licensed data, he responded: “Well, that’s the majority of what we’re using, for sure.”
paragraphs.zh: 6 月，在英国的审判接近尾声时，Getty 在一项更广泛的诉讼中撤回了版权侵权指控，公司取得了重大胜利。美国的审判仍在进行中。阿卡拉朱表示，公司“在训练和微调时使用公开可得与授权的数据集”，并且在“为客户打造解决方案”时，它“会使用由客户提供的数据集进行微调”。当我问阿卡拉朱该公司是否只使用免费或有授权的数据进行训练时，他回答道：“当然，绝大多数我们使用的都是这些来源。”

paragraphs.en: Even those who are bullish on AI admit that, for the most part, the technology isn’t ready for the big screen. Text-to-image generators might work for marketing agencies, but they often lack the quality required for a feature film. “I worked on one film for Netflix and tried to use a single shot,” says a filmmaker who asked to remain anonymous, not wanting to discuss their use of AI publicly. The AI-generated footage got “bounced back” from quality control because it wasn’t 4K resolution, the filmmaker says.
paragraphs.zh: 即使是那些看好人工智能的人也承认，在大多数情况下，这项技术还没准备好登上大银幕。文本生成图像工具或许能用于广告公司，但它们往往达不到故事片所需的质量。一位不愿公开谈论自己使用人工智能的匿名电影制作人说：“我为 Netflix 做过一部电影，尝试过只用一个镜头。”他说，这段由人工智能生成的素材在质检环节被“打回”，因为它不是 4K 分辨率。

paragraphs.en: Then there’s the problem of consistency. Filmmakers need to be able to tweak a scene in minute ways, but that’s not possible with most of the image and video generators on the market. Enter the same prompt into a chatbot 10 times and you will likely get 10 different responses. “That doesn't work at all in a VFX workflow,” Cameron says. “We need higher resolution, we need higher repeatability. We need controllability at levels that aren't quite there yet.”
paragraphs.zh: 此外还有一致性问题。电影人需要能够对一个场景进行非常细微的调整，但市场上大多数图像和视频生成器都无法做到这一点。把同一个提示词在聊天机器人里连续输入 10 次，你很可能会得到 10 个不同的结果。卡梅隆说：“这在 VFX 工作流里根本行不通。我们需要更高的清晰度，需要更高的可重复性。我们需要的是更高水平的可控性，而这些目前还不够成熟。”

paragraphs.en: That hasn’t stopped filmmakers from experimenting. Almost every person I spoke with for this story said that AI is already a core part of the “previz” process, where scenes are mapped out before a shoot. The process can create new inefficiencies. “The inefficiency in the old system was really the information gap between what I see and what I imagine I want moving forward,” says Luisa Huang, cofounder of Toonstar, a tech-forward animation company. “With AI, the inefficiency becomes ‘Here's a version, here's another version, here's another version.’”
paragraphs.zh: 这并没有阻止电影人进行实验。在我采访的几乎所有人中，都表示人工智能已经成为拍摄前期“预可视化（previz）”流程的核心部分，这个流程会在开拍前先把场景进行梳理和构建。该流程也会带来新的低效。托恩斯塔（Toonstar）这家技术驱动动画公司的联合创始人卢伊萨·黄（Luisa Huang）说：“旧系统中的低效，实际上是我眼前所见与我脑海中想要实现内容之间的信息鸿沟。‘有了 AI，这种低效变成了：这是一个版本，这是另一个版本，这是又一个版本。’”

paragraphs.en: One of the first people in Hollywood to admit to using generative AI in the final frame is Jon Erwin, the director and producer of Amazon’s biblical epic House of David. He became interested in the technology while shooting the first season of the show in Greece. “I noticed that my production designer was able to visualize ideas almost in real time,” he says. “I was like, ‘Tell me exactly how you’re doing what you’re doing. What are you using, magician?’” he recalls.
paragraphs.zh: 好莱坞最早公开承认在最终画面中使用生成式 AI 的人之一是乔恩·欧文（Jon Erwin），他是亚马逊圣经史诗电影《大卫之屋》（House of David）的导演兼制片人。他在希腊拍摄该剧第一季时对这项技术产生了兴趣。欧文说：“我注意到我的美术指导几乎能实时把想法可视化。”他回忆道：“我当时想：‘告诉我你到底是怎么做到的，你都在用什么，像魔法师一样？’”

paragraphs.en: Erwin started playing around with the tools himself. “I felt directly tethered to my imagination,” he says. Eventually, he made a presentation for Amazon outlining how he wanted to use generative AI in his production. The company was supportive.
paragraphs.zh: 欧文后来自己开始尝试这些工具。“我觉得我和自己的想象力之间建立了直接连接，”他说。最终，他为亚马逊做了一个提案，说明他希望如何在自己的制作中使用生成式 AI。公司表示支持。

paragraphs.en: “We film everything we can for real—it still takes hundreds of people,” Erwin tells me. “But we’re able to do it at about a third of the budget of some of these bigger shows in our same genre, and we’re able to do it twice as fast.” A burning-forest scene in House of David would have been too expensive to do with practical effects, he says, so AI created what audiences saw.
paragraphs.zh: 欧文对我说：“我们能真实拍摄的东西都会拍，毕竟仍需要成百上千人来完成。”但在他看来，这种方式可以把预算压缩到同类题材更大制作片的约三分之一，而且速度可以快一倍。“《大卫之屋》里的一场燃烧森林戏份，用真实特效去做成本会非常高”，因此就由 AI 来创造观众最终看到的画面。

paragraphs.en: Erwin says he has spoken with the team at Stability but has “not been able to use their tools successfully on a show at scale.” The comment reflects a theme I found in my reporting: While I was able to identify a number of filmmakers who admitted to toying around with Stability’s text-to-image generators, none used the tools professionally—at least not yet.
paragraphs.zh: Erwin 说他已经与 Stability 团队交谈过，但“尚未能在一档节目中大规模成功使用他们的工具”。这番话反映了我在报道中发现的一个主题：虽然我识别出不少电影人承认自己玩过 Stability 的文字转图像生成器，但至少到目前为止，还没有人把这些工具用于职业化制作。

paragraphs.en: Contains AI-generated imagery.
paragraphs.zh: 包含人工智能生成的影像。

paragraphs.en: The taboo on studios acknowledging their embrace of AI seems to be softening. In July, Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos told investors the company had allowed “gen AI final footage” to appear in one of its original series for the first time. He said the decision sped up production tenfold and dramatically cut costs. “We remain convinced that AI represents an incredible opportunity to help creators make films and series better, not just cheaper,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: 制片厂公开承认自己正在采用 AI 的禁忌似乎正在放松。7 月，Netflix 联合首席执行官 Ted Sarandos 告诉投资者，公司第一次允许“生成式 AI 的最终镜头”出现在其一部原创剧集中。他说这一决定使制作速度提升了十倍，并大幅降低了成本。Sarandos 说：“我们始终确信，AI 为创作者提供了一个难得的机会，不仅是为了更便宜地制作电影和剧集，更是为了把它们做得更好。”

paragraphs.en: Hanno Basse, Stability’s chief technology officer, is showing me an image of his backyard in Los Angeles: a grassy lawn surrounded by high hedges, rose bushes crowding a bay window, and a tree in the far left-hand corner. Suddenly, the 2D image unfurls into 3D. A generative AI model has filled in the gaps, estimating depth (how far away the hedge is from the rose bush, the tree from the window) and other missing elements to make the scene feel immersive. Basse can replicate camera moves by selecting from a drop-down menu: zoom in or out, pan up or pan down, spiral.
paragraphs.zh: Stability 的首席技术官 Hanno Basse 正在向我展示他在洛杉矶后院的一张图片：一片草坪被高高的树篱环绕，玫瑰花丛挤在一个三角窗前，左前方角落里有一棵树。突然，这张 2D 图像展开成 3D。一个生成式人工智能模型补齐了空缺部分，推测出深度（树篱与玫瑰丛相距多少、树与窗户相隔多少）以及其他缺失元素，使画面更具沉浸感。Basse 可以通过下拉菜单复现摄影机运动：放大或缩小，向上摇摆或向下摇摆，乃至做螺旋运动。

paragraphs.en: “Instead of spending hours or days or weeks building a virtual environment and rehearsing your shots, the idea here is actually that you can just take a single image and generate a concept,” Basse says.
paragraphs.zh: “与其花几小时、几天甚至几周去搭建虚拟场景并排练镜头，不如说这里的想法实际上是：你只需要拿一张图片，就能生成一个概念，”贝斯说。

paragraphs.en: Contains AI-generated imagery.
paragraphs.zh: 包含 AI 生成的图像。

paragraphs.en: Rob Legato, Stability’s chief pipeline architect, seems pleased. A veteran visual effects specialist who worked on Wolf of Wall Street and Avatar, Legato joined the company in March. He was up until 2 am the night before shooting a film and has arrived at this meeting to act as both a company executive and a beta tester.
paragraphs.zh: Rob Legato 这位稳定扩散公司的首席制作流程架构师似乎很满意。他是一位曾参与《华尔街之狼》和《阿凡达》的资深视觉特效专家，于三月加入该公司。前一晚他熬到凌晨两点还在拍电影，后来却来到这次会议，既以公司高管身份出席，又兼任测试版用户。

paragraphs.en: The only issue, Legato says, is the drop-down menu. “You probably want to combine them and have a slider,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: Legato 说，唯一的问题是下拉菜单。“你可能想把它们合并起来，并用一个滑块来控制，”他说。

paragraphs.en: Contains AI-generated imagery.
paragraphs.zh: 包含人工智能生成的图像。

paragraphs.en: Stability AI’s offerings are still in their early days. Even Legato admits the version of the virtual camera tool we are looking at has a ways to go before it could be used by a professional. “Right off the bat my job is unfortunately to be critical,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: Stability AI 的产品仍处于早期阶段。就连 Legato 也承认，我们现在看到的这款虚拟摄像机工具在成为专业可用工具之前，还有不少距离。“一上来我的工作不幸地就是要挑剔它，”他说。

paragraphs.en: The conversation drifts to rotoscoping. Legato explains that this process, where an artist sketches over a scene frame by frame, used to take hundreds of hours and was reserved for entry-level animators. Now AI can automatically isolate part of an image and add visual effects. “You’d never want your child to work on roto,” he tells me.
paragraphs.zh: 谈话很快转向了抠像（roto）技术。Legato 解释说，这一流程过去需要艺术家逐帧勾画场景，通常要花费数百小时，并且多被分配给初级动画师。如今，AI 可以自动分离图像的一部分并添加视觉特效。“我会劝你千万不要让你的孩子去做 roto，”他对我说。

paragraphs.en: The comment is meant to sound optimistic, but it gets to a looming fear about how AI will impact Hollywood. Namely, that the technology will lead to widespread job losses.
paragraphs.zh: 这番话本意是要表达乐观，但它触及了一个逐渐逼近的担忧，即 AI 对好莱坞的影响。也就是说，这项技术可能会带来大规模的就业流失。

paragraphs.en: “I hear artists at VFX companies say, ‘Hey, I don't want to get replaced.’ Of course you don't want to get replaced!” says Cameron. “If you guys are going to lose your jobs, you're going to lose your jobs over the work drying up versus getting bumped aside by these gen AI models.” The idea, echoed by Akkaraju and Parker, is that as movies become cheaper to produce, more films will get made and overall employment will rise.
paragraphs.zh: “我听到一些特效公司里的艺术家说：‘嘿，我不想被取代。’ 当然你不想被取代，”Cameron说。“如果你们要失去工作，那也不是因为这些生成式AI模型把你们挤走，而是因为电影工作本身变少，岗位在萎缩。Akkaraju和Parker都反复强调这个观点：因为电影制作成本更低，会拍出更多电影，总体就业反而会上升。”

paragraphs.en: When pressed on this point, Akkaraju reverts to an extended metaphor. “Every major transition or technological invention is always met with apprehension at first, and then acceptance, and then it's obvious,” he says. “When ATMs rolled out in the ’80s, all the tellers were really up in arms. They were like, ‘That’s our job. We give withdrawals, we take deposits, and now you’re having this machine do it.’ What’s happened since then is that there are more teller jobs than ever before, and their average pay is higher, even adjusted for inflation.”
paragraphs.zh: 在被追问到这一点时，Akkaraju又用了一个更长的比喻。“每一次重大转型或技术发明，起初总是伴随着担忧，后来才是接受，最终变得显而易见，”他说。“20世纪80年代自动取款机（ATM）开始普及时，柜员们当时都很激烈地反对。他们会说：‘那是我们的工作。我们负责取款和存款，现在你却让机器来做了。’自那以后，事实是柜员岗位数量反而创下历史新高，而且即便按通胀调整后，他们的平均工资也更高。”

paragraphs.en: Whether the coup that began in Lady Gaga’s greenhouse ultimately saves Stability AI, the AI revolution is here and already transforming Hollywood. That collapsing building, that burning forest, that crowd of people you see when you stream a show or go to the movie theater? One person with a keyboard could’ve made them. The thing about that bank-teller anecdote is that it’s often used by techno-optimists—including Stability AI investor Eric Schmidt. What they don’t mention is that the number of bank tellers peaked around 2015. Since then, it’s been on the decline.
paragraphs.zh: 无论从Lady Gaga的温室里开始的那场“政变”最终是否挽救了Stability AI，AI革命已经到来，并且正在改变好莱坞。那栋坍塌的大楼、那片燃烧的森林、你在追剧或去电影院时看到的人群？一个拿着键盘的人就能把它们都做出来。关于银行柜员的这类例子，常常被技术乐观主义者拿来引用——包括Stability AI的投资人Eric Schmidt在内。它们没有提到的是：银行柜员人数在2015年前后达到高峰，此后一直在下降。”

paragraphs.en: Update: 8/20/2025, 4:45 PM EDT: WIRED has corrected the spelling of Jon Erwin's name. WIRED has also clarified details about Stability AI's training data, as well as its investors, and removed a reference to Stable Diffusion 1.5.
paragraphs.zh: 更新：2025年8月20日，东部夏令时下午4:45：WIRED已更正了Jon Erwin姓名的拼写。WIRED还澄清了关于Stability AI训练数据以及其投资人的细节，并删除了对Stable Diffusion 1.5的一处引用。

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article. Submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 请告诉我们您对本文的看法。请向编辑部来信：[email protected]。
